purpose: Per-parser-family AST-shape contract: assert the runtime AST that the generated regex parser actually produces, and surface drift between the grammar's declared return annotations and what the running parser emits. A failing assertion here means either (a) the running parser changed shape (regenerate intended? update the manifest in the same commit) or (b) a previously documented drift was fixed (flip drift_status to `aligned` and update current_content_kind).
doctrine: The 'declared_annotation' field is the source of truth from grammars/regex.ebnf. The 'expected_content_kind' field is the shape we expect once the parser correctly applies that annotation. The 'current_content_kind' field is what the tracked generated/regex_parser.rs emits today. When current != expected, drift_status names the open work needed to align them.

samples.name: regex_simple_alternation
samples.drift_tracked_in: PGEN: per-family regex regeneration follow-up requires RGX coordination before tracked generated/regex_parser.rs is regenerated; regex_parser_integration_contract 1.1.31 will need a version bump and required_rule_names refresh when the typed-Json carrier reaches the public API.
